jokes.text: You're such a dildo, dude. - Thanks, dude.
jokes.setup: Mac insults Dennis by calling him a dildo
jokes.punchline: Dennis responds with sincere gratitude as if it was a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and subversion of expected reaction. Dennis's oblivious acceptance shows his narcissism.

jokes.text: Is that coffee gonna help you forget that elephant you slept with last night?
jokes.setup: Dennis is judging Mac's health drink choice
jokes.punchline: Dennis fires back with a cruel insult about Mac's sexual partner
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's petty cruelty but relies on basic fat-shaming humor

jokes.text: 'Cause I don't know what kind of diseases that girl had.
jokes.setup: Mac wants to try Dennis's drink after Dennis made fun of it
jokes.punchline: Dennis refuses because he's worried about diseases from Mac's sexual partner
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cruelty and shows Dennis's paranoia about contamination

jokes.text: A B&E? - What does that mean?
jokes.setup: Charlie announces there's been a B&E
jokes.punchline: Dennis doesn't know basic police terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's privileged ignorance of criminal terminology

jokes.text: We need to buy a gun.
jokes.setup: The group is trying to figure out how to respond to being robbed
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately jumps to the most extreme solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's solution to any problem is violence. Sets up the entire episode.

jokes.text: I think next time you boys ought to get the kind of safe that a relatively small man can't just carry away.
jokes.setup: Police are investigating the robbery
jokes.punchline: Officer delivers extremely obvious advice in a condescending tone
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of painfully obvious advice, highlighting police incompetence

jokes.text: Not really.
jokes.setup: Asked if they have any leads on a series of robberies
jokes.punchline: Completely unhelpful non-answer delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective portrayal of police indifference

jokes.text: Remember the other day when you were making fun of us for having such a tiny safe?
jokes.setup: Mac is explaining the robbery to Colin
jokes.punchline: References Colin previously mocking their inadequate safe security
jokes.explanation: Plants a clue about Colin's involvement while highlighting their poor security

jokes.text: He's like a full-on '80s movie stereotype.
jokes.setup: Discussing their hatred of Colin
jokes.punchline: Charlie reduces Colin to a specific cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and accurate pop culture reference that nails Colin's character type

jokes.text: Mac gets a boner every single time he thinks about a gun.
jokes.setup: Dee asks if Mac and Dennis will really buy a gun
jokes.punchline: Charlie describes Mac's obsession in crude sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Mac's gun obsession with vivid imagery. Charlie's blunt observation style.

jokes.text: Actually, it really turns me on.
jokes.setup: Colin is describing guns as sexy
jokes.punchline: Dee admits to sexual arousal from gun use
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's willingness to be inappropriate to seem cool to Colin

jokes.text: You've got a poppy seed in your teeth.
jokes.setup: Dee and Colin are having a sexually charged conversation
jokes.punchline: Colin breaks the mood with an unsexy observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect mood killer that deflates Dee's attempt at seduction. Great comedic timing.

jokes.text: I cannot wait anymore!
jokes.setup: Charlie owes Hwang rent money
jokes.punchline: Hwang's broken English makes his anger sound more desperate than threatening
jokes.explanation: Relies on accent-based humor which feels dated and cheap

jokes.text: Hwang not patient!
jokes.setup: Hwang is angry about overdue rent
jokes.punchline: Speaks about himself in third person with broken grammar
jokes.explanation: Continues the problematic accent-based humor pattern

jokes.text: Would you like a Rice Krispie treat?
jokes.setup: Hwang is demanding $854 in rent money
jokes.punchline: Charlie offers a children's snack as if it's a reasonable alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie logic. The complete mismatch between problem and solution is brilliant.

jokes.text: I placed a couple bad investments!
jokes.setup: Charlie owes massive rent money
jokes.punchline: Describes his financial irresponsibility as sophisticated investing
jokes.explanation: Charlie's delusional reframing of obvious poor choices as business decisions

jokes.text: You wanna shoot it?
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis have just bought a gun for protection
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately wants to use it recreationally instead of storing it safely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac moment - can't resist the gun's appeal despite safety concerns

jokes.text: How do you like me now, log?
jokes.setup: Dennis is shooting at a log for target practice
jokes.punchline: Taunts an inanimate object as if it were a sentient enemy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dennis's need to dominate everything, even inanimate objects

jokes.text: I always knew that I'd be good at this stuff.
jokes.setup: Dennis hits a stationary log a few times
jokes.punchline: Claims natural talent based on minimal evidence
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissism shows in claiming innate skill at shooting stationary targets

jokes.text: Charlie, my boy, you've got the gun fever.
jokes.setup: Charlie reluctantly holds the gun but gets excited
jokes.punchline: Mac diagnoses Charlie with the episode's titular condition
jokes.explanation: Episode title drop with perfect delivery. Mac as the gun fever expert is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: once you put your hand around this... you are gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Colin is teaching Dee to shoot
jokes.punchline: Uses sexual innuendo when talking about guns
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo but fits Colin's sleazy character

jokes.text: the gun's not here.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis want to go shoot more
jokes.punchline: Discovery that Charlie has taken the gun
jokes.explanation: Good plot escalation that raises stakes and tension

jokes.text: I just needed it.
jokes.setup: Charlie is asked why he took the gun
jokes.punchline: Vague, ominous non-explanation delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Perfectly ominous Charlie response. The vagueness makes it more threatening.

jokes.text: I have my ways.
jokes.setup: Charlie is telling Hwang he'll get the rent money somehow
jokes.punchline: Vague threat while clearly holding a gun
jokes.explanation: Perfectly menacing Charlie moment. The visual of him holding a gun makes the threat clear.

jokes.text: Sometimes I can't understand you. You're talking, but I don't understand you.
jokes.setup: Charlie is trying to intimidate Hwang
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't understand Hwang's accent while holding him at gunpoint
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Charlie being racist while committing a crime. Uncomfortably funny.

jokes.text: We're like the Hardy Boys.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis are planning to investigate the robbery themselves
jokes.punchline: Compares their criminal investigation to children's mystery books
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that highlights their childish approach to serious crime

jokes.text: 'Cause Frank was kind of the leader, and that makes a little more sense...
jokes.setup: Mac wants to assign Hardy Boys roles
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets into detailed argument about fictional character hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's need to be the alpha even in children's book roleplay

jokes.text: I hope Charlie didn't do anything stupid. - Whatever Charlie's doing right now, it's probably pretty stupid.
jokes.setup: Dee expresses concern about Charlie's actions
jokes.punchline: Dennis matter-of-factly assumes Charlie is doing something stupid
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Dennis's casual dismissal of Charlie reflects their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: Colin.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks who robbed their bar
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals their suspect with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing for the reveal. Single word punchline with maximum impact.

jokes.text: Move. Oh, this place is a shit-hole, Charlie. It smells.
jokes.setup: Mac enters Charlie's apartment
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults Charlie's living conditions
jokes.explanation: Basic Mac rudeness. Shows his lack of tact but nothing special.

jokes.text: I'll slide the gun meaningfully into the front of my pants...
jokes.setup: Mac is planning their confrontation strategy
jokes.punchline: Describes putting gun in pants as if it's a sophisticated intimidation tactic
jokes.explanation: Mac's naive understanding of intimidation tactics. The word 'meaningfully' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It goes... Uh, stick it back in...
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to demonstrate his gun intimidation technique
jokes.punchline: Struggles with basic gun handling while trying to look cool
jokes.explanation: Classic gap between Mac's self-image and reality. Physical comedy of incompetence.

jokes.text: What a penis!
jokes.setup: Mac fumbles with the gun demonstration
jokes.punchline: Dennis uses an oddly childish insult
jokes.explanation: Dennis using juvenile language despite trying to seem sophisticated

jokes.text: Do you think this guy would be dumb enough to rob the same bar twice?
jokes.setup: They realize Colin is at Fingers Bar which was already robbed
jokes.punchline: Dennis questions if someone would be stupid enough to rob the same place twice
jokes.explanation: Ironic since they're about to do something equally stupid. Sets up their plan backfiring.

jokes.text: We actually keep it in the register instead of the safe.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis are trying to lure Colin into robbing them again
jokes.punchline: Claims they keep money in the most obvious place possible
jokes.explanation: Their plan is so obvious it's painful. Shows their lack of criminal sophistication.

jokes.text: What a cock! - What an idiot! What an idiot! What a total idiot, right?
jokes.setup: They're calling the robber stupid for not checking the register
jokes.punchline: Increasingly excited pile-on of insults gets carried away
jokes.explanation: Their enthusiasm for the insults shows how caught up they get in their own performance

jokes.text: Maybe you guys should get a gun.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis have been obviously baiting him about the robbery
jokes.punchline: Colin suggests they get protection, not knowing they already have a gun
jokes.explanation: Good irony since they already have a gun and plan to use it on him

jokes.text: Did you just cock back the hammer, bro?
jokes.setup: They're waiting in the dark to catch Colin
jokes.punchline: Mac notices Dennis is getting trigger-happy
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis getting carried away with the gun power. Builds tension.

jokes.text: Shoot him in his face!
jokes.setup: They spot someone in the bar during their stakeout
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately escalates to execution
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Mac's violence. The casual way he says 'in his face' is chilling and funny.

jokes.text: I'm shot! - Charlie? Oh, my God! - Did you shoot me, Dennis? - Why did you shoot me?
jokes.setup: Dennis fires the gun in the dark
jokes.punchline: He accidentally shoots Charlie instead of Colin
jokes.explanation: Major escalation and perfect incompetence from Dennis. Dark but perfectly executed mistake.

jokes.text: I'm shot!
jokes.setup: Dennis fires the gun in the dark
jokes.punchline: Charlie announces he's been shot
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy payoff. Their incompetence leads to shooting their friend instead of the burglar.

jokes.text: Charlie, I shot you in your head. I am so sorry.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes he shot Charlie
jokes.punchline: Delivers devastating news with inappropriate casualness
jokes.explanation: Dennis's matter-of-fact delivery of horrific news is perfectly in character and darkly funny.

jokes.text: If I shot him in the head, he'd be dead.
jokes.setup: Everyone is panicking about Charlie being shot in the head
jokes.punchline: Dennis uses simple logic to downplay the severity
jokes.explanation: Dennis's sociopathic logic applied to attempted murder. Perfectly disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: I don't wanna get blood stains all over the interior of my car.
jokes.setup: Charlie needs to go to the hospital after being shot
jokes.punchline: Dennis prioritizes his car's cleanliness over his friend's life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism. Caring more about car upholstery than a friend's gunshot wound is peak terrible person behavior.

jokes.text: We'll put it over him.
jokes.setup: Dennis agrees to drive Charlie but wants to protect his car
jokes.punchline: Suggests putting a trash bag over the gunshot victim
jokes.explanation: Treating Charlie like garbage literally. The visual of a trash bag over a gunshot victim is darkly absurd.

jokes.text: You shot me, dude.
jokes.setup: Charlie is in the hospital after being shot
jokes.punchline: States the obvious with understated disappointment
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual delivery of being shot by his friend. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You shot me, dude. - But, Charlie, you were stealing from the bar. It was an honest mistake.
jokes.setup: Charlie points out he was shot
jokes.punchline: Dennis calls shooting his friend an 'honest mistake' because Charlie was technically stealing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis rationalization - shooting someone is an 'honest mistake' if they were technically doing something wrong. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: I was gonna make it look like all the other robberies going on around town. Then we would collect some insurance money
jokes.setup: Charlie explains why he stole from his own bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals an elaborate insurance fraud scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - stealing from himself for insurance money. Shows his warped problem-solving.

jokes.text: It might be the best idea you've ever had, but...
jokes.setup: Charlie's insurance fraud scheme has been revealed as terrible
jokes.punchline: Mac accidentally compliments Charlie while trying to criticize him
jokes.explanation: Mac accidentally admits Charlie's past ideas were even worse than insurance fraud

jokes.text: Tell me we're not getting rid of that gun.
jokes.setup: They've just agreed to get rid of the gun after Charlie was shot
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately wants to keep the gun despite the obvious danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac moment - learning nothing from nearly killing his friend. Gun fever remains strong.

jokes.text: Idiots.
jokes.setup: Colin has been listening to Dee's call about the gang's incompetent adventure
jokes.punchline: Single word dismissal of their entire scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy. Colin's assessment is completely accurate and delivered with appropriate disdain.
